title: Epipactis decipiens
taxonomy: Epipactis decipiens is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is further classified as belonging to the genus Epipactis. The genus Epipactis is a group of orchids commonly known as helleborines. Within the Orchidaceae family, Epipactis decipiens is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is one of the largest and most diverse groups within the Orchidaceae family.
uses: Epipactis decipiens, also known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a type of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Its roots and rhizomes are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications. Further studies are required to confirm the efficacy and safety of Epipactis decipiens as a medicinal plant.

intro: Epipactis decipiens, also known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Epipactis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis decipiens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the autumn lady's tresses is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is commonly used to treat respiratory conditions and as an antispasmodic agent.
